channel.title: The Moonshroud Realms
channel.description: Recent content on The Moonshroud Realms

item.title: Algar's Central Coast
item.description:
Algar Central Coast#

A regional map of Algar’s central coast, showing the lands, settlements, and geography familiar to anyone who has grown up in this part of the world.

item.title: Bridgeport
item.description:
Bridgeport#

Bridgeport is a freehold city of approximately 8,000 souls situated at the mouth of the Godsfall River where it empties into the Zephyrys Sea at Pell’s Harbor, on the western coast of Algar. It is the realm’s second-largest seaport and a critical hub between the northern heartlands and the wider world. Merchant cogs, river barges, and distant ocean vessels crowd its piers year-round.

It is a city of commerce, ambition, and barely contained chaos — a place where fortunes are made and lost, where every faction has a foothold, and where the streets themselves seem to have opinions.

item.title: Council of Trades
item.description:
Council of Trades#

The Council of Trades is the governing body of Bridgeport — a assembly of merchant representatives whose authority derives from commerce rather than birthright. In Bridgeport, coin is power, and the Council is where that power is formally organized.

The Council manages the city’s laws, its markets, its harbor fees, and its relationships with the Crown and neighboring settlements. It is not a democracy in any simple sense — representation on the Council is tied to trade volume and guild membership — but it is at least predictable, which counts for a great deal in a city like Bridgeport.

item.title: The Gods
item.description:
The Gods#

The divine order of the Moonshroud Realms flows from a single source: Spherus, the creator, who imagined nine Eternals into being at the founding of the world. The Eternals are not gods in the conventional sense — they are fundamental forces given will and purpose, each responsible for a domain of reality rather than a nation of worshippers.

Beneath the Eternals exist the Ascendants — beings of mortal origin who were elevated to divinity through extraordinary deeds, divine selection, or profound sacrifice. Ascendants are more accessible to mortal understanding than the Eternals, and their stories are told in temples, around fires, and in the songs of traveling bards.

item.title: Council Members
item.description:
Council of Trades — Members#

The Council of Trades is drawn from Bridgeport’s ten most influential guilds and professional orders. Each guild elects a Guildmaster-Delegate to serve a five-year term. Together they govern the Freehold, with one member serving as High Convenor — currently Seraphina Velcross of the Merchants’ Exchange.

What follows is what a reasonably informed Bridgeport resident would know about the people who run the city.

Seraphina Velcross#

High Convenor — Merchants’ Exchange

item.title: The Moons
item.description:
The Seven Moons of Orb#

The world of Orb turns beneath seven moons. Every person who has ever looked up at a clear night sky in the Moonshroud Realms has seen them — some bright, some faint, some moving faster than others across the dark. Most people know them as a fact of life, the way they know the seasons or the tides. Scholars and druids know them as something more.

item.title: West Bridgeport
item.description:
West Bridgeport City Map#

A map of West Bridgeport, the freehold city at the mouth of the Godsfall River.

item.title: Wexford
item.description:
Wexford#

Wexford is a walled woodland town of approximately 1,000 souls, situated some fifty miles south of Bridgeport in the depths of the Wexwood Forest. It sits at the confluence of the Wex River and Spring Creek, and is predominantly home to the Aelfaen — though travellers, traders, and those with business in the forest pass through regularly enough that outsiders are not unknown.

It is a quieter place than Bridgeport, but quieter does not mean simple.

item.title: Adventurer's Guild
item.description:
Adventurer’s Guild#

The Adventurer’s Guild is one of the few organizations that operates openly across most of the known world, maintaining halls in cities and towns large enough to support one. Bridgeport’s chapter is well-established, reasonably well-funded, and busy enough that its staff have developed the particular brand of weary pragmatism common to people who spend their days sending others into danger.

What the Guild Does#

The Guild serves as a clearinghouse for work — connecting those with problems requiring unconventional solutions to those willing to provide them, for appropriate compensation. It posts contracts, verifies completion, handles payment disputes, and maintains records of members in good standing.

item.title: Funnis
item.description:
Funnis#

Funnis is a Woodlands Ursinor settlement roughly one hundred miles east of Bridgeport, nestled deep within the Whistle Woods where the eastern reaches of the Sanctum Hills give way to the lower slopes of the Livingstone Mountains. It sits on a low plateau overlooking Clear Creek, a fast-flowing river that cuts through a shallow ravine before turning south toward the Godsfall.

It is not a city. It is not a town in the Bridgeport sense. It is a community — self-sufficient, deliberate, and entirely on its own terms.

item.title: The World Beyond
item.description:
The World Beyond Bridgeport#

Bridgeport sits at the mouth of the Godsfall River on the western coast of Algar, one nation among many on the continent of Elderland. It is a busy, prosperous city — and it is also a very small part of a very large world.

The Continent of Elderland#

Elderland is the known world for most of its inhabitants. It is vast enough that few people ever see more than a fraction of it in a lifetime, and large enough that its eastern reaches are more legend than geography to those living on the western coast.

item.title: Wexford
item.description:
Wexford Town Map#

A map of Wexford, the Aelfaen woodland town fifty miles south of Bridgeport in the Wexwood Forest.

item.title: Royal Adventuring Society
item.description:
Royal Adventuring Society#

The Royal Adventuring Society — known commonly as the R.A. Society — is the Crown’s elite counterpart to the Adventurer’s Guild. Where the Guild handles the broad spectrum of sanctioned adventuring work, the Society directs missions that are too specialized, too sensitive, or too politically complicated for a military detachment or a standard Guild contract.

These assignments tend toward the discreet: recoveries that cannot be public, investigations requiring subtlety, negotiations where a regiment would be wasteful or diplomatically disruptive, and threats where the Crown needs capable hands that don’t wear a uniform.

item.title: The Kangs
item.description:
The Kangs#

Bridgeport has neighborhoods where the city’s official rules are more suggestion than law. In those places, other arrangements apply.

The Kangs are a presence in certain parts of the city. Anyone who spends time on Bridgeport’s streets will notice them eventually — groups moving with purpose, wearing a single item in a consistent color. Red. Blue. Yellow.

What that means, exactly, is the kind of thing most people in Bridgeport have learned not to ask too loudly.
